findings.evidence: `Trivial.lean:64` defines `instGroupLike`, `:98` defines `IsCoinvariantFor`, `:139` defines `ofCoinvariantFor`, and `Comodule.lean:92` gives the regular comodule; Mathlib `Coalgebra/GroupLike.lean:66` provides `GroupLike`, so no `[Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]` is needed until specializing to `1` via `Bialgebra/GroupLike.lean:27`.
findings.fix: Add a coalgebra-level `groupLikeToSelf (g : GroupLike R C)` sending `r` to `r • (g : C)` (or derive it via `ofCoinvariantFor`), then define `baseToSelf` as the specialization to `(1 : GroupLike R C)`.
findings.issue: `baseToSelf` proves only the `1 : C` bialgebra case of the canonical morphism from a `g`-trivial comodule to the regular comodule.

summary: One remaining API is still specialized to the bialgebra unit where the natural coalgebra-level group-like statement is available from the surrounding development.
